title: Autranella letestui
taxonomy: Autranella letestui is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Sapotaceae. It is a member of the genus Autranella, which is a group of tropical trees native to the forests of Central Africa. The plant is also known as the 'African Sapote'. The species was named after the French botanist and explorer Pierre Letestu. Autranella letestui is considered to be an endangered species due to habitat loss and other environmental factors.
uses: Autranella letestui, a plant species native to the Congo region, has been used in traditional medicine for various purposes. Its roots and leaves are believed to possess anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. In folk medicine, the plant is used to treat various ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions. Additionally, Autranella letestui is also believed to have antimalarial properties, making it a potential natural remedy for malaria. However, more research is needed to fully understand and confirm its medicinal uses.

intro: Autranella letestui, also known as "Ntoba Nkui" or "Okoume tree," is a plant species native to Central Africa, belonging to the family Annonaceae and being a shrub or small tree with edible fruit and medicinal properties. The plant has been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and skin infections, due to its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties. The bioactive constituents of Autranella letestui include alkaloids, flavonoids, and terpenes, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, Autranella letestui is often used to make herbal preparations such as decoctions, infusions, and ointments to treat various health ailments.
